title: 10 best herbal creams for lower back pain

remedy_properties: CBD: CBD has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with lower back strain or injury.
THC: THC has natural muscle relaxant properties, which can help to ease muscle spasms and tension in the lower back, providing relief from chronic discomfort and stiffness.

Terpenes (such as myrcene and limonene): Terpenes have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back conditions like herniated discs or sciatica.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa, also known as marijuana, creams helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds, such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the lower back. By targeting specific pain receptors, these creams can provide long-lasting relief and improve mobility, making everyday activities more manageable and comfortable. This natural remedy has been shown to be an effective alternative to traditional pain management methods.
remedy_constituents: Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Cannabigerolic Acid (CBGA): CBGA has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate lower back pain by reducing inflammation and pain signaling.

Beta-Caryophyllene (BCP): BCP has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain, as well as promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain cannabinoids like CBD, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help reduce lower back pain.
Buds: They have high concentrations of cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, which are effective in reducing pain and inflammation in the lower back.
Flowers: They are a rich source of cannabinoids and terpenes, which work together to provide pain relief and reduce inflammation in the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Infuse a mixture of 1 part dried cannabis sativa flowers and 2 parts carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 2-3 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Combine 1 part of the infused oil with 2 parts of beeswax and 1 part of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted and well combined then remove from heat.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains salvenic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it effective in relieving lower back pain caused by inflamed muscles and joints.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to chronic inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate lower back pain and improve overall joint health.

Muscle relaxant compounds: Sage contains flavonoids such as apigenin, which have been shown to have a muscle-relaxing effect, helping to reduce muscle spasms and stiffness in the lower back region, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Salvia officinalis, such as thujone and camphor, have been found to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms, providing relief from lower back pain. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of sage may help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area, making it a popular natural remedy for lower back pain sufferers. Its soothing effects also promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the lower back.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and pain associated with lower back pain.

Salvianolic acids: These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic activities, which help reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and pain associated with lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to soothe and relieve pain in the lower back area.
Roots: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate lower back pain.
Stems: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks or until brittle.
Combine 1 part dried salvia officinalis with 2 parts base oil such as coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease pain in the lower back, making it a natural alternative to nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs).

Pain-relieving: The topical application of arnica cream helps block the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that transmit pain signals to the brain, providing fast and effective relief from lower back pain.
Antispasmodic: Arnica's antispasmodic properties help relax muscle spasms in the lower back, reducing stiffness and discomfort, and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, relieving tension and pressure on the muscles and joints. This allows for improved blood flow and relaxation of the muscles, providing long-lasting relief from lower back pain. Additionally, Arnica's analgesic properties numb the pain, providing instant relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Thiophene Derivatives: These compounds, particularly sesquiterpene lactones, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the lower back.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Arnica montana creams, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.

Alkamides: Alkamides in Arnica montana creams have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate lower back pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Arnica flowers are used to make creams for lower back pain because they contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Arnica leaves are used to make creams for lower back pain due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Arnica stems are used to make creams for lower back pain because they contain a mixture of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh arnica montana flowers and 1/2 cup of dried flowers for future use.
Dry the fresh flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until brittle.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried arnica montana flowers 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of coconut oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil to the cooled mixture and stir.

remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): Helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system.
Capsaicin: Relieves pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Arnica oil: Reduces inflammation and relieves pain by improving blood circulation and reducing swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with lower back pain because it contains capsaicin, a natural compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P. Substance P is responsible for transmitting pain signals to the brain. When applied topically, capsaicin in the creams creates a sensation of heat, which in turn reduces inflammation and relaxes muscles, providing relief from lower back pain. Regular use can also improve circulation and reduce pain associated with arthritis and fibromyalgia.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a vanilloid compound found in Capsicum annuum, helps with lower back pain by activating TRPV1 receptors, which reduces the release of pain-producing substances and increases the release of pain-relieving substances in the body.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Capsicum annuum, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate lower back pain.

Carotenoids: Carotenoids like beta-carotene and lycopene in Capsicum annuum have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to relief from lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They contain capsaicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the lower back area.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum also contain capsaicin, which can be extracted and used to create creams for pain relief.
Seeds: The seeds contain capsaicin as well, making them a potential source for creating creams to alleviate lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature capsicum annuum fruits, wash them clean with lukewarm water, and chop them finely.
Combine 1/2 cup of chopped capsicum annuum, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted completely, in about 10-15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat, let it cool slightly, then blend the mixture using an immersion blender until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar, store it in the refrigerator, and allow it to chill for at least 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, providing relief from lower back pain.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic properties of ginger creams help to numb the sensation of pain, reducing discomfort and stiffness associated with lower back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Ginger creams contain compounds that can help relax tense muscles, improve blood flow, and reduce muscle spasms, all of which contribute to relieving lower back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce swelling and relax tense muscles. This can help alleviate pressure on the lower back, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, ginger creams may also help reduce muscle spasms and improve blood flow to the affected area, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects. This can lead to improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to their ability to alleviate lower back pain by blocking pain transmission and reducing inflammation.

Curcuminoids: Although not exclusively found in ginger, curcuminoids are sometimes present in Zingiber officinale creams. These polyphenolic compounds possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate lower back pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the underground stems of the plant, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce pain and inflammation.
Roots: Similar to rhyzomes, roots of ginger contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe lower back pain and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant are also used to make creams for lower back pain, as they contain compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried zingiber officinale root and grind it into fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 50g of beeswax pellets and 20g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 20g of coconut oil and 5g of vitamin e oil to the double boiler and stir until combined for 5 minutes.
Stir in 10g of the ground zingiber officinale root powder and 5g of shea butter into the mixture for 2 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before pouring it into small containers for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow cream contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the muscles and joints.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's analgesic properties help numb the affected area, reducing discomfort and pain, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation that can contribute to lower back pain.

Topical delivery system: When applied topically, white willow cream allows for a targeted and localized delivery of the active ingredients, reducing systemic absorption and potential side effects while providing effective relief from lower back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with lower back pain because it contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. This allows it to provide effective pain relief and reduce inflammation in the affected area, thereby alleviating lower back pain. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Salix alba creams also help to relax tense muscles and improve mobility, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals suffering from lower back pain. Regular application can lead to sustained relief.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin is a phenolic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate lower back pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain receptors.
Tannins: Tannins are a type of polyphenol that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation associated with lower back pain.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, are antioxidants that possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate lower back pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of muscles.
remedy_parts: Barks: Salix alba barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Leaves: Salix alba leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing and relaxing the muscles to alleviate lower back pain.

Buds: Salix alba buds are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain, as well as their ability to stimulate blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salix alba bark in late spring or early summer when the sap is rising.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm dark place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture.
Create a decoction by combining 50 grams of dried salix alba bark with 500ml of water.
Simmer the decoction for 10 minutes then strain it using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix 10ml of the cooled decoction with 20ml of a neutral oil base and 5 grams of beeswax.

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, a group of compounds found in turmeric, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with lower back pain.

Corticosteroid-like properties: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has been shown to mimic the actions of corticosteroids, reducing swelling and inflammation in the body, which can provide relief from lower back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or sciatica.

Pain-relieving compounds: Turmeric contains other bioactive compounds like gingerols and sesquiterpenes that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to numb pain receptors and reduce discomfort associated with lower back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in the joints and muscles, making it an effective natural remedy for lower back pain. The cream can penetrate deep into the skin to target the root cause of the pain, providing quick relief and reducing stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with lower back pain.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a demethoxylated analog of curcumin, has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may contribute to its potential pain-relieving properties in lower back pain.
Ar-turmerone: Ar-turmerone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa in creams for lower back pain due to their high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: Roots are also widely used in creams for lower back pain, as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain.
Barks: Barks are sometimes used in creams for lower back pain, as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried turmeric rhizomes and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the dried turmeric rhizomes to the double boiler and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of essential oil.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and then pour it into a glass jar.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a major active compound in St John's Wort that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with lower back pain.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in St John's Wort creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to chronic lower back pain.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in St John's Wort creams have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve muscle spasms and tension associated with lower back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in the muscles and joints. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows for direct absorption into the affected area, providing targeted relief from lower back pain. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate various types of pain, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones (Hyperforin and Hypericin): They help alleviate lower back pain by modulating neurotransmitter activity and influencing pain perception in the brain, providing a potential analgesic effect.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These compounds contribute to reducing inflammation and modulating pain response, which may help alleviate lower back pain caused by inflammation or oxidative stress.

Phenolic Acids (Chlorogenic Acid and Cryptochlorogenic Acid): They help alleviate lower back pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain response through their antioxidant properties and potential inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are commonly used to make creams for lower back pain because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are used in creams for lower back pain due to their content of hyperforin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Hypericum perforatum are also used in creams for lower back pain because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom, ideally in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve.
Mix 200 grams of beeswax, 200 grams of coconut oil, 50 grams of shea butter, and 10 grams of the powdered hypericum perforatum in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 160-180 degrees fahrenheit, stirring occasionally, and allow it to cool and solidify before scooping into jars.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains eucalyptol and other volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, thereby alleviating lower back pain.

Analgesic and numbing properties: The menthol content in Tasmanian Blue Gum creams can provide a topical analgesic effect, numb the pain and discomfort, and relax tense muscles, making it an effective relief for lower back pain.

Muscle-relaxant properties: The cineole present in the cream has been shown to have muscle-relaxing effects, which can help reduce muscle spasms, stiffness, and tension in the lower back region, leading to a reduction in pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cooling effect of eucalyptus oil can help reduce muscle spasms and relax tense muscles, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, making it an effective remedy for lower back pain. Its topical application can provide fast and long-lasting relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene acts as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, alleviating lower back pain.
Beta-caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back region.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate lower back pain.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its pain-relieving effects in creams.

Essential Oil from Leaves: Although leaves are listed separately, the essential oil from leaves is a concentrated form of the active compounds and is often used in creams for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 4-6 hours or until the oil has turned a light green color.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a glass container to remove the eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter to the oil and heat the mixture until the wax has melted.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and allow it to cool and solidify before use.

remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew cream containing parthenolide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce swelling and relieve pain in the lower back.

Feverfew's sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to inflammation and pain in the body, helping to alleviate lower back discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Feverfew cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic pain and inflammation in the lower back.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tanacetum parthenium, also known as feverfew, creams helps with lower back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Tanacetum parthenium, such as parthenolide, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the lower back region. The cream also has a cooling effect on the skin, providing temporary relief from discomfort and stiffness. Additionally, Tanacetum parthenium creams may help relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, further alleviating lower back pain and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with lower back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and pain in the affected area.
Tanetin: This flavonoid derivative has anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate lower back pain by reducing swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve lower back pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in compounds like tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: Contain parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that exhibits anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects.
Stems: May contain similar compounds to the leaves and flowers, contributing to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon to minimize essential oil loss.
Dry the harvested plant material at 40 degrees celsius for 12 hours to preserve its active properties completely.
Steep 10 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil for 2 weeks to extract essential oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract solution.
Mix the herbal extract with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil to create a smooth and stable cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with lower back pain because it provides a natural and effective way to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to cool the skin, reducing heat and discomfort associated with lower back pain. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to soothe and calm the area, reducing stiffness and promoting flexibility. This combination of anti-inflammatory and relaxing benefits makes herbal peppermint creams a popular natural remedy for alleviating lower back pain.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints, providing relief from lower back pain.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant that can help relieve muscle spasms and stiffness associated with lower back pain by reducing swelling and promoting blood flow to the affected area.
Volatile oils (such as limonene and beta-pinene): These oils have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles, reduce tension, and alleviate chronic lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the lower back.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, measuring 20 grams for this recipe, on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm oven at 180°f for 6 hours, stirring occasionally.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with 200ml of cold-pressed olive oil and 10 drops of vitamin e oil in a glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by storing it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily, until the oil turns greenish-blue.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and add 100ml of beeswax, heating at low heat stirring constantly until melted and mixed well.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with lower back pain because of its natural ability to numb and relax muscles, reducing tension and discomfort. The methyl salicylate in wintergreen has analgesic properties that help reduce inflammation and ease stiffness, providing relief from aching muscles and joints. Additionally, the cooling sensation of the cream can also help to calm and soothe the affected area, allowing for improved mobility and reduced pain, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating lower back pain.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol provides cooling relief to the muscles and joints, helping to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the lower back.

Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and stiffness in the lower back by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation.

Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate is a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce swelling and ease pain in the muscles and joints of the lower back, similar to aspirin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain high concentrations of methyl salicylate, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and stiffness in the lower back.
Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat musculoskeletal pain, including lower back pain, due to their ability to penetrate deep into tissue and provide relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry gaultheria procumbens leaves according to traditional methods for optimal potency and quality.
Grind dried gaultheria procumbens leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 10 grams of ground gaultheria procumbens with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and continue heating for an additional 15 minutes stirring until fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the cream base into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with lower back pain because it has a calming effect on the muscles, reducing inflammation and tension. The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender soothe sore joints and muscles, relaxing the body and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, the cream's cooling sensation can help reduce heat and swelling associated with lower back pain, providing welcome relief from stiffness and soreness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with lower back pain, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving pain and inflammation.

Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and numbing effects of lavender oil help block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing temporary relief from acute or chronic lower back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Lavender's sedative and relaxing compounds help calm tense muscles in the back, reducing muscle spasms and stiffness, which can contribute to lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate lower back pain.
Leaves: Containing terpenes and sesquiterpenes, these compounds can be extracted to produce analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects on the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 2 tablespoons.
Combine the lavender powder with 6 tablespoons of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar and store it at room temperature for 4 to 6 weeks to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids and discarding the solids after 2 hours.
Combine the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until melted.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into glass jars or tubes, allowing it to solidify at room temperature for about an hour before labeling and storing in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with lower back pain because they contain natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that effectively soothe and relax the muscles. The cream's active ingredients, such as geraniol and citral, work together to reduce muscle spasms and ease tension in the lower back region. This results in a significant reduction of pain and discomfort, allowing individuals to move freely without restriction. Additionally, geranium creams can also help to improve circulation and reduce stiffness, promoting overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Geranium oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with lower back pain, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of geranium oil work to numb the pain and discomfort in the lower back, providing relief from chronic aches and stiffness.
Muscle relaxant: Geranium oil's ability to relax muscles helps reduce muscle spasms and tension that can contribute to lower back pain, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the geranium plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with lower back pain.
Rhyzomes: Geranium rhizomes contain bioactive compounds that have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and relieve lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried pelargonium graveolens leaves and stems in a sterile environment weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine the dried herb with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 10 percent beeswax pellets by weight and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is fully melted and the temperature reaches 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture into a smooth creamy texture using an electric mixer or whisking by hand.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with lower back pain because its natural anti-inflammatory properties soothe and relax tense muscles. The cream's active compounds, such as 1,8-cineole and borneol, work together to reduce swelling and discomfort in the lower back area. As a result, marjoram creams can help alleviate chronic pain and stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals experiencing lower back pain. Additionally, its warming sensation can also help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sweet marjoram cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as thymol and borneol, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with lower back pain.

Analgesic properties: The essential oils present in sweet marjoram, particularly linalool and sabinene, have analgesic properties that help to numb the affected area, providing relief from acute and chronic lower back pain.

Muscle relaxant effects: Marjoram's camphor content helps to relax muscles, which can reduce muscle spasms and tension that contribute to lower back pain, allowing for improved flexibility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, which help soothe muscle spasms and reduce inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help relax muscles and calm discomfort in the lower back region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried origanum majorana leaves in sufficient quantities for the recipe.
Grind the dried origanum majorana leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground origanum majorana with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture while stirring until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass containers and let it cool before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle spasms that often contribute to lower back pain. When applied topically, these oils can penetrate deep into the skin, providing targeted relief to sore muscles and joints. Additionally, thyme creams may also help relax tense muscles, promoting improved blood flow and reduced stiffness in the lower back region.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, alleviating lower back pain.
Pain-relieving properties: Thyme contains analgesic compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have a numbing effect on nerve endings, providing relief from chronic lower back pain.
Antispasmodic properties: Thyme's antispasmodic compounds, such as terpenoids and flavonoids, help relax muscle spasms and cramps in the lower back, reducing stiffness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have a rich concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle spasms associated with lower back pain.
Barks: The barks of the thyme plant contain volatile oils that provide anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing joint pain and stiffness in the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems, then clean them thoroughly.
Combine the herbs with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar, storing it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 80 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat and add 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil, stirring well before pouring into sterilized containers and allowing to cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that target the affected area, reducing inflammation and swelling. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-caryophyllene, penetrate deep into the skin to reach the muscles and joints, providing fast relief from discomfort. Additionally, rosemary's analgesic properties help numb the pain, allowing individuals to move with greater ease and flexibility, while its antioxidant properties promote faster healing and tissue repair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints associated with lower back pain.
Pain relief: The menthol and camphor present in rosemary creams have a cooling effect, which can help numb the affected area and provide temporary pain relief from lower back pain.
Muscle relaxant: Rosemary's antispasmodic properties, including its ability to relax muscle fibers, can help reduce muscle spasms and stiffness often associated with lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of camphor, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with lower back pain.
Barks: Rosemary barks have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce muscle tension and alleviate back pain symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Combine the dried leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar and store it for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids, and transfer the infusion to a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the infusion in the double boiler and stir until the wax is fully incorporated.
Remove from heat, let cool, and whip with a spatula or whisk until the cream reaches a smooth consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet creams helps with lower back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which target the affected area and provide relief from discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, including salicin and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and relax muscles, easing tension in the lower back region. By reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles, meadowsweet creams can help alleviate chronic pain and stiffness associated with lower back conditions, promoting a sense of comfort and mobility.
remedy_properties: Salicylic acid: Meadowsweet creams contain salicylic acid, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the lower back.

Glycosides: The glycosides present in meadowsweet cream have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints, reducing lower back pain.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids found in meadowsweet cream have been demonstrated to exhibit analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to relieve muscle spasms and stiffness that contribute to lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with lower back pain.
Barks: Contain mucilage, a soothing substance that can help calm irritated skin and relieve discomfort caused by inflammation in the lower back.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of filipendula ulmaria flowers in the shade within 24 hours.
Mix the dried flowers with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to create a smooth consistency.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal solids after cooling slightly.
Transfer the final cream to clean glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with lower back pain because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to provide relief from muscle and joint discomfort. Pain-relieving essential oils: The essential oils present in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease lower back pain and discomfort.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the lower back.
Rhyzomes: Contain oils and compounds that provide analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing lower back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours at room temperature to allow for extraction of essential oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 20% of the extracted oil with 80% of a moisturizing cream base such as petroleum jelly and whip until smooth.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for lower back pain are topical remedies containing plant extracts that help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with lower back issues. These creams are effective due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The best herbal creams for lower back pain include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Capsicum annuum, Arnica montana, Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cannabis sativa, Salix alba, Vitis vinifera, and Melaleuca alternifolia. If left untreated, lower back pain can negatively impact daily life, causing fatigue, limited mobility, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting mental health and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lower back pain that are treated with herbal creams are muscle and joint soreness, arthritis, and sprains. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, St. John's Wort, and capsaicin have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain, inflammation, and stiffness in these areas. These creams can be applied topically to provide localized relief without the need for oral medication or injections.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with lower back pain is a blend of Arnica, CBD, and Ginger. Arnica reduces inflammation and promotes healing, while CBD provides relief from chronic pain and discomfort. Ginger cream enhances blood circulation and eases muscle tension. Apply a layer of Arnica cream first, followed by CBD and finally Ginger cream. Leave it on for 30 minutes to an hour before showering. This combination can help alleviate lower back pain and promote relaxation.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, herbal creams containing Harpagophytum Procumbens, Salix Alba, and Capsicum Frutescens may be beneficial for lower back pain. This conclusion is based on ten trials included in the review, where two high-quality trials found that daily doses of Harpagophytum Procumbens, standardized to 50 mg or 100 mg harpagoside, were better than placebo for short-term improvements in pain and rescue medication. Additionally, two trials found that daily doses of Salix Alba, standardized to 120 mg or 240 mg salicin, were better than placebo for short-term improvements in pain and rescue medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with lower back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in Curcuma longa has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the lower back. Additionally, Curcuma longa creams promote blood circulation, reducing stiffness and promoting healing, making it a popular natural remedy for lower back pain. Zingiber officinale creams helps with lower back pain because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that target and reduce inflammation in the lower back muscles and joints. Regular application of these creams may also help to reduce pain and stiffness in the lower back, providing long-lasting relief and improved mobility. Capsicum annuum creams helps with lower back pain because they contain capsaicin, a natural analgesic that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P. Substance P transmits pain signals to the brain, and by blocking it, capsaicin reduces pain perception. The cream is applied topically to the affected area, where it is absorbed and works to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing quick and effective relief from lower back pain. This natural remedy offers a welcome alternative to traditional pain medications.

When applied topically, Arnica montana creams can help to relax tense muscles, improve blood flow, and reduce pain and stiffness in the lower back. This natural remedy can provide relief from chronic or acute lower back pain, promoting relaxation and flexibility in the affected area. Regular use may also improve overall muscle health.
